_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Encouraging and building mutual trust, respect, and cooperation among team members.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in building and leading high-performing data and AI teams, including role definition, hiring strategy, onboarding design, performance management, and creating team cultures that balance technical excellence with business orientation.
comments: Near-exact match. WIDAI LS-S-004 is the domain-specific expression of team development. Strong overlap in trust-building, culture creation, and team performance.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score for an Equal relationship reflects vocabulary divergence between O*NET's general-purpose language and WIDAI's domain-specific terminology. The concepts are equivalent despite surface-level phrasing differences that compress the cross-encoder score.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8668 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. The P/R asymmetry for an Equal relationship suggests one description is more specific or uses more distinctive vocabulary than the other, even though the underlying competency is judged equivalent.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (98.41%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral for an Equal pair reflects vocabulary divergence rather than conceptual divergence — the NLI model processes surface language, and cross-domain terminology differences suppress the entailment signal even when the underlying concepts align.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.4136) meets or exceeds the STS cross-encoder (0.3648). This is uncommon and suggests the semantic similarity is well-represented in the embedding space — the descriptions occupy similar vector-space regions even without joint processing.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (8.33% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.

signals.detail: STS 0.365 barely clears classification threshold for Equal. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
